title: Two Sum
description: Find pair that sums to target

data.content:
# Two Sum

Given an array of integers `nums` and an integer `target`, return indices of the two numbers that add up to `target`.

## Example
```
Input:  nums = [2, 7, 11, 15], target = 9
Output: [0, 1]
```

## Constraints
- Exactly one valid solution
- 2 ≤ nums.length ≤ 10⁴

## Hash Map Approach (O(n))
Store seen values; for each element check if complement exists.

title: Sliding Window Maximum Sum
description: Maximum sum subarray of fixed size K

data.content:
# Sliding Window Maximum Sum

Find the maximum sum of any contiguous subarray of size `k`.

## Example
```
Input:  nums = [2, 1, 5, 1, 3, 2], k = 3
Output: 9
```
The window slides right by one each step, adding the new element and removing the leftmost.

## Complexity
- Time: O(n)
- Space: O(1)

# Binary Search

Find target value in sorted array in O(log n).

Divide the array in half each iteration, discarding the half that cannot contain the target.

## Key Points
- Array must be sorted
- Three pointers: left, mid, right
- Terminates when left > right

## Complexity
- Time: O(log n)
- Space: O(1)

title: Valid Palindrome
description: Check if string reads same forward and backward

data.content:
# Valid Palindrome

A phrase is a palindrome if it reads the same forward and backward after converting to lowercase and removing non-alphanumeric characters.

## Example
```
Input:  "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama"
Output: true
```

## Approach
Two pointers from both ends, skip non-alphanumeric, compare lowercase.

tests.input: ["A man, a plan, a canal: Panama"]

title: Valid Anagram
description: Check if two strings are anagrams

data.content:
# Valid Anagram

Given two strings `s` and `t`, return true if `t` is an anagram of `s`.

## Example
```
Input:  s = "anagram", t = "nagaram"
Output: true
```

## Approach
Count character frequencies. Both strings must have the same count for each character.

title: Longest Substring
description: Find longest substring without repeating characters

data.content:
# Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters

Given a string `s`, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.

## Example
```
Input:  "abcabcbb"
Output: 3 ("abc")
```

## Approach
Sliding window with a hash set. Expand the right pointer; if a duplicate is found, shrink from the left until the duplicate is removed.

# Bubble Sort

Repeatedly step through the array, compare adjacent elements, and swap if they are in the wrong order.

Each pass bubbles the largest unsorted element to its correct position.

## Example
```
Initial: [5, 1, 4, 2, 8]
Pass 1:  [1, 4, 2, 5, 8]
Pass 2:  [1, 2, 4, 5, 8]
```

## Complexity
- Time: O(n²) worst, O(n) best (optimized)
- Space: O(1)

# Merge Sort

Divide the array into halves, recursively sort each half, then merge the sorted halves.

## Key Insight
A single-element array is always sorted. Build up from there.

## Complexity
- Time: O(n log n)
- Space: O(n) auxiliary

title: Quick Sort
description: Divide and conquer with pivot partitioning

data.content:
# Quick Sort

Pick a pivot, partition the array around the pivot, and recursively sort the partitions.

## Partitioning (Lomuto)
Elements smaller than the pivot go left, larger go right. The pivot ends up in its correct position.

## Complexity
- Time: O(n log n) average, O(n²) worst
- Space: O(log n) stack

# Contains Duplicate

Given an integer array `nums`, return true if any value appears at least twice.

## Example
```
Input:  [1, 2, 3, 1]
Output: true
```

## Approach
Use a Set. For each element, if already in the set, a duplicate was found.

title: Maximum Subarray (Kadane)
description: Find contiguous subarray with largest sum

data.content:
# Maximum Subarray (Kadane's Algorithm)

Find the contiguous subarray with the largest sum.

## Example
```
Input:  [-2, 1, -3, 4, -1, 2, 1, -5, 4]
Output: 6 (subarray [4, -1, 2, 1])
```

## Kadane's Insight
At each position, decide: extend the current subarray or start fresh?
`currentSum = max(nums[i], currentSum + nums[i])`

# Climbing Stairs

You climb a staircase with `n` steps. Each step you can go up 1 or 2 steps. How many distinct ways to reach the top?

## Example
```
Input:  n = 3
Output: 3
```
Ways: (1,1,1), (1,2), (2,1)

## Recurrence
`dp[i] = dp[i-1] + dp[i-2]`
This is the Fibonacci sequence.

## Complexity
- Time: O(n)
- Space: O(1)
